content:
3 min read 1 hour ago -- 1. Introduction In the previous article, we learned how to use Child Routes to organize related pages under a parent route. In this article, we’ll learn about Route Guards, specifically the CanActivate guard. A CanActivate guard is used to control access to a route. It checks whether a user is allowed to visit a particular page before Angular loads it. 2. What is CanActivate? CanActivate is a route guard that decides whether a route can be activated. If it returns: • true → Navigation is allowed. • false → Navigation is blocked. It is commonly used to protect pages that require the user to be logged in. 3. What We Will Build Press enter or click to view image in full size We’ll create a simple application with: • Home Page • Login Page • Dashboard Page (Protected) Behavior: • User can open Home and Login. • Dashboard is protected. • If the user is not logged in, Angular redirects them to the Login page. 4. Create Components ng generate component home ng generate component login ng generate component dashboard5. Create CanActivate Guard ng generate guard authChoose: CanActivateAngular creates: auth.guard.ts6. Create Authentication Service ng generate service authauth.service.ts import { Injectable } from '@angular/core';@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' }) export class AuthService { isLoggedIn = false; } This service stores whether the user is logged in. 7. Implement the Guard auth.guard.ts import { inject } from '@angular/core'; import { CanActivateFn, Router } from '@angular/router'; import { AuthService } from './auth.service';export const authGuard: CanActivateFn = () => { const auth = inject(AuthService); const router = inject(Router); if (auth.isLoggedIn) { return true; } router.navigate(['/login']); return false; }; Explanation • Check if the user is logged in. • If yes → allow navigation. • Otherwise → redirect to Login page. 8. Configure Routes app.routes.ts import { Routes } from '@angular/router';import { HomeComponent } from './home/home.component'; import { LoginComponent } from './login/login.component'; import { DashboardComponent } from './dashboard/dashboard.component'; import { authGuard } from './auth.guard'; export const routes: Routes = [ { path: '', component: HomeComponent }, { path: 'login', component: LoginComponent }, { path: 'dashboard', component: DashboardComponent, canActivate: [authGuard] } ]; Definition The canActivate property tells Angular to execute the guard before opening the route. 9. Login Component login.component.ts import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { Router } from '@angular/router'; import { AuthService } from '../auth.service';@Component({ selector: 'app-login', standalone: true, templateUrl: './login.component.html' }) export class LoginComponent { constructor( private auth: AuthService, private router: Router ) {} login() { this.auth.isLoggedIn = true; this.router.navigate(['/dashboard']); } } 10. Login HTML

Login Page

11. Dashboard HTML

Dashboard

Welcome! You have successfully logged in.

12. App Navigation app.component.html
Home Login Dashboard
13. Import Required Modules app.component.ts import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { RouterLink, RouterOutlet } from '@angular/router';@Component({ selector: 'app-root', standalone: true, imports: [RouterLink, RouterOutlet], templateUrl: './app.component.html' }) export class AppComponent { } 14. How It Works Case 1: User is not logged in User opens /dashboard↓ Guard executes ↓ auth.isLoggedIn↓ Returns false↓ Angular redirects to /loginCase 2: User clicks Login ↓ login() method executes ↓ auth.isLoggedIn = true;↓ Navigate to /dashboard↓ Guard executes again ↓ Returns true↓ Dashboard opens successfully. 15. Flow Diagram User Opens Dashboard │ ▼ CanActivate Guard Executes │ ▼ Is User Logged In? │ │ Yes No │ │ ▼ ▼ Open Dashboard Redirect to Login16. Why do we use CanActivate? • Protect private pages. • Prevent unauthorized access. • Improve application security. • Control navigation before loading a component. 17. Summary In this article, we learned: • What a Route Guard is. • What CanActivate does. • How to create a guard. • How to protect routes using canActivate. • How to redirect users to the Login page when they are not authenticated.
